physics_sources.cluster_scaling: sigma_v = sqrt(G*M/r_vir) [virial self-similar limit; cf. Evrard 2008]

conclusions: k_A(z) propto H(z)^(4/3) — monotonically increasing with z by virial theorem + overdensity criterion.
r_A(z) = n^(-1/3) — monotonically increasing with z (fewer clusters at higher z).
r_P(z) = r_A/(1+z) — shape depends on competition between cluster depletion and (1+z) factor.
D_C:AB = r_A (minimal assumption) — monotone by construction.
Best positive Pearson r across all MAVS components and M_min variants: -0.257.
Secondary eps structure at z=1.0-1.5 NOT reproduced by any individual MAVS component.
AUTHOR_SCHEDULE_NEEDED: virial+PS MAVS is CONDITIONAL RECONSTRUCTION — not the author's method.
<HYPOTHESIS>: k_A from virial theorem connects cluster kinetic energy to H_MULT scale — unconfirmed without bridge formula (Q1).

k_A(z) from virial theorem scales as H(z)^(4/3) — monotone. r_A(z) scales as n(z)^(-1/3) — monotone. r_P(z) = r_A/(1+z) may have extremum but is dominated by high-z divergence. None of the MAVS components reproduces the non-monotonic eps(z) structure. Q2 blocker confirmed: author-specific k_A(z) / D_eff(z) schedule essential.
